message:
style(server): rustfmt reflow of mail_ui test fixture + tui_screens attachment width/height parsing

Pure rustfmt wrap updates across two files.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/mail_ui.rs

- The recently-added
  render_attachments_preserves_messages_when_agent_rows_are_missing
  test and its sibling render_message assertion block contained
  two format! calls that exceeded rustfmt's default printWidth:
    format!("/tmp/mail-ui-attachments-orphaned-agents-{}", unique_nonce())
  and the call chain building the rendered HTML from
  render_message(&cx, &pool, &project.slug,
  message.id.unwrap_or(0)).  Both are now wrapped across
  multiple lines per rustfmt's standard rules.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/attachments.rs

- The width/height parsing chain inside the attachment-browser
  loader previously fit on a single long line:
    let width = att.get("width").and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64).unwrap_or(0) as u32;
  rustfmt wants each method call in the chain on its own line
  when the expression is this long, producing:
    let width = att
        .get("width")
        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64)
        .unwrap_or(0) as u32;
  Same treatment applied to the sibling `let height = ...`
  expression.

No functional change — the #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
attributes and the actual logic (extract u64, default to 0,
truncate to u32) are preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -364,13 +364,15 @@ impl AttachmentExplorerScreen {
                             .unwrap_or("")
                             .to_string();
                         #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
-                        let width =
-                            att.get("width").and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64).unwrap_or(0)
-                                as u32;
+                        let width = att
+                            .get("width")
+                            .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64)
+                            .unwrap_or(0) as u32;
                         #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)]
-                        let height =
-                            att.get("height").and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64).unwrap_or(0)
-                                as u32;
+                        let height = att
+                            .get("height")
+                            .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_u64)
+                            .unwrap_or(0) as u32;
                         let mode = att
                             .get("type")
                             .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_str)
